test: Capacity Scaling — deposit ops/sec as capacity grows
note: 50K ops never exceeds 100K capacity — pop(0)/shift never called at 100K. At 1K/10K, trimming happens frequently.
key_finding: At 100K capacity with 50K ops: NO trimming triggered = no pop(0)/shift cost. Languages show different behaviors because the trimming frequency varies. Rust VecDeque is the only O(1) structure that maintains performance as capacity grows.

interpretation.python_10K_slowness: list.pop(0) O(n) shifting happens ~40K times at 10K capacity = ~20K element shifts per operation
interpretation.go_1K_slowness: slice[1:] creates new backing array copy each trim, constant at small capacity
interpretation.go_100K_fast: No trimming at 100K — slice grows from 0 to 50K, no copy needed
interpretation.rust_consistency: VecDeque ring buffer handles all cases without performance cliff

degradation_summary.Rust: 1.9x (most consistent, slight ring buffer overhead growth)
